pi_items.source: GeoServer environment setup (scripts/setup_geoserver.sh, line 136) - workspace name established in task environment

Natural Earth dataset imported as PostGIS table. Filename 'ne_110m_admin_0_countries.shp' imported as table 'ne_countries' (setup_geoserver.sh line 114). Natural Earth Admin 0 Countries is a real public dataset containing 258 countries including Brazil and Egypt (naturalearthdata.com/downloads/110m-cultural-vectors/110m-admin-0-countries/)
Custom style name specified in task.json and export_result.sh. This is the name to be created in GeoServer. Consistent throughout task definition (task.json line 19, export_result.sh line 14)
Environment variable parameter name specified in task.json metadata and referenced in export verification script (export_result.sh lines 15, 51, 73, 132, 145). Consistent throughout task definition

Standard hex color code for pure red. Verified: R=255 (0xFF), G=0 (0x00), B=0 (0x00). Export verification script validates this color by checking R>150 AND G<100 AND B<100 (export_result.sh line 104). This hex code is correct

Standard hex color code for gray. Verified: R=204 (0xCC), G=204 (0xCC), B=204 (0xCC). Export verification script validates this color by checking |R-G|<40 AND |G-B|<40 (export_result.sh line 123). This hex code is correct and represents a neutral gray

Output file path specified in task.json metadata (line 23), task description (line 5), and export_result.sh (line 16). Consistent throughout task definition. File is checked for existence and creation timestamp in export script (lines 27-32)

privileged_info_summary: The task uses the real, publicly available Natural Earth Admin 0 Countries dataset (ne_110m_admin_0_countries, version 5.1.1) which contains 258 countries including Brazil and Egypt. This is not synthetic data but sourced from naturalearthdata.com. The task is configured to create a GeoServer SLD style named 'dynamic_country' in the 'ne' workspace, applied to the 'ne_countries' layer. The style must use the 'env' SLD function with parameter 'target_country' to dynamically highlight countries in red (#FF0000 - verified as pure red RGB(255,0,0)) while rendering all others in gray (#CCCCCC - verified as neutral gray RGB(204,204,204)). The verification script tests with Brazil and Egypt as target cases, validating color rendering through WMS requests with different env parameter values. The expected output file path is /home/ga/highlight_brazil.png.
